scenario: Mastodon work session - audit the Status contract before a teardown rework

map_quality.rationale: Hands the agent a clear map: Rails 8 + Sidekiq + React frontend + streaming, with app/ subdirs (models/services/workers/lib/policies/serializers) and points to Status at app/models/status.rb:34 as the hub. Could have been slightly sharper on which lib/ pieces matter most (FeedManager named, good).

specificity.rationale: Concrete paths throughout: `app/models/status.rb:34`, `app/models/concerns/status/*`, `app/lib/FeedManager`, `app/workers/activitypub/`, `spec/fabricators/`, not vague layer talk.

justification.rationale: Explains Status as 'the hub feeds, notifications, federation, moderation, search and trends all reference' and notes behavior split via concerns, but the WHY is asserted briefly rather than developed (e.g., why services vs workers split).

uncertainty.rationale: No hedges or flags about anything unconfirmed; the answer is presented as fully certain even though it covers a broad layout.

relationship.rationale: States connecting relationships at a high level: behavior 'built on shared concerns', Status is the 'hub feeds/notifications/federation/moderation reference', workers/activitypub/ for federation delivery. But it doesn't name HOW feeds reference statuses or how moderation acts on them beyond the hub assertion.

steps.step: Map the Status contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: Comprehensive map with file:line for the model, every concern, every association with dependent: option, and teardown-specific callbacks/scopes — agent could change the contract with only small targeted lookups.

specificity.rationale: Every concern is cited by file:line (e.g., `app/models/concerns/status/visibility.rb:3`, `threading_concern.rb:24,:28`), associations cited at `status.rb:75-118` with exact lines, no vague 'the concerns' phrasing.

justification.rationale: Explains WHY pieces matter (e.g., `default_scope { recent.kept }` is 'the single most load-bearing fact for a teardown rework'; SafeReblogInsert blocks reblogs of discarded statuses; unlink_from_conversations must run before mentions wipe). Could go deeper on why behavior is split across concerns architecturally.

uncertainty.rationale: No explicit flags on parts the answer couldn't confirm; presents everything with equal confidence. Paginable is cited without a line, but not flagged as unconfirmed.

relationship.rationale: Each contract piece is bound to Status by stated relation: concerns 'included' with what each supplies (SearchConcern → indexable/searchable_by, ThreadingConcern → ancestors/descendants CTEs, Visibility → enum + scopes), associations named with their dependent: option (nullify vs destroy vs delete), and callbacks tied to lifecycle hooks.

steps.step: Trace how a status is created and distributed

map_quality.rationale: Traces the full chain with file:line from PostStatusService entry through persistence callbacks, postprocess dispatch, local fan-out (DistributionWorker → FanOutOnWriteService → FeedInsertWorker → FeedManager), and federation (ActivityPub::DistributionWorker → StatusReachFinder → DeliveryWorker), explicitly marking inline vs async. An agent could act with only tiny targeted lookups.

specificity.rationale: Every layer is named with concrete path and line: `app/services/post_status_service.rb:42`, `app/workers/distribution_worker.rb:8`, `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12`, `app/workers/feed_insert_worker.rb:7`, `app/lib/feed_manager.rb:76,102`, `app/workers/activitypub/distribution_worker.rb:11`.

justification.rationale: Shows WHY each pipeline step exists by labeling the role (persistence, post-processing, local fan-out, federation) and inline vs async, but doesn't explicitly articulate WHY distribution is pushed async (fan-out scale / per-recipient cost) beyond the implicit redis-lock note.

uncertainty.rationale: Splits clearly into inline vs async lists, which is a form of bounded confidence, but does not flag any sub-step that wasn't fully opened (e.g., what DeliveryWorker does downstream, list-membership resolution inside FeedInsertWorker).

relationship.rationale: Explicitly assembled as a chain with arrows: `PostStatusService` → callbacks → `postprocess_status!` → `DistributionWorker` → `FanOutOnWriteService` → `FeedInsertWorker` → `FeedManager#push_to_home/list`, and federation chain `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker` → `StatusReachFinder` → `DeliveryWorker`. The connecting relations (redis-locked, per-recipient, inboxes from reach finder) are stated.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Status contract

map_quality.rationale: Exhaustively enumerates dependents across every requested area (feeds, federation, notifications, moderation, account lifecycle, import/export/backup, background maintenance) with file:line, plus a bonus section on association-tear-down models and a serialization surface. Hits the rubric's named non-obvious targets (feed_manager, permalink_redirector implied via status_finder, backup_service, annual_report, account warnings).

specificity.rationale: Nearly every dependent is named at file:line with a short phrase on usage (e.g., 'app/services/backup_service.rb:37 outbox statuses, :154 favourite_statuses', 'app/lib/status_reach_finder.rb:3'). A handful of entries in sections E/F/H lack line numbers, but the great bulk are precise.

justification.rationale: Each section is grouped by area with a why-it-matters phrase per entry ('fans a status to all local feeds/streams', 'snapshots, media, poll'). It doesn't always articulate the broader area-level motivation, but per-item justification is consistently present.

uncertainty.rationale: Does not flag what a static pass might miss or which non-obvious dependents (concern-reached, association-reached, derived) the author is least sure about; no caveats anywhere.

relationship.rationale: Many entries state the relation route: 'belongs_to :activity polymorphic', 'has_one :notification, as: :activity', 'has_many :statuses' on account concern, FetchRepliesConcern, custom_filter_status via status_id FK, search_concern. However, scattered consumers (annual_report, backup, permalink/status_finder) are mostly listed as endpoints without an explicit chain like 'reached through X association/concern', so the chain assembly is partial.

steps.step: Trace how status identity and visibility are guarded

map_quality.rationale: Comprehensive map across discard/existence guards (default_scope kept, with_discarded, RecordNotFound rescues, race condition) and visibility/authorization (StatusPolicy, StatusFilter, FeedManager, StatusReachFinder, FanOutOnWriteService) with file:line throughout — agent could navigate with only small follow-ups.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every guard is anchored with file:line (e.g. `status.rb:128`, `fan_out_on_write_service.rb:29`, `status_policy.rb:4`, `feed_manager.rb:49`) and the specific predicate/scope named.

justification.rationale: Explains WHY guards exist in key places (`with_discarded` is the 'deliberate opt-out where teardown must see dead rows', race condition re-queues on nil visibility, DB-level no reblog of discarded), though some entries are listed without rationale.

uncertainty.rationale: Answer asserts confidently throughout; no explicit flagging of implicit guards, gaps, or paths the author was unsure about.

relationship.rationale: Guards are connected to the dependents they protect: StatusFilter→feeds via FeedManager#filter, StatusReachFinder gates federated deletes, FanOutOnWriteService#broadcastable? gates public streams, ThreadingConcern applies StatusFilter across threads — paths are stated rather than methods in isolation.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: Comprehensive blast radius across teardown, federation, feeds, search/trends, FASP, counter caches, moderation, backup, and visibility, with file:line and a clear CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM ranking. Minor gaps: annual reports and permalink redirector aren't called out, but the structural coverage is strong.

specificity.rationale: Dependents named concretely with file:line (e.g., `safe_reblog_insert.rb:20`, `status.rb:89-118`, `batched_remove_status_service.rb:34`, `delete_account_service.rb:160-205`) and each has a stated reason for its risk tier.

justification.rationale: Each tier explains WHY: `SafeReblogInsert` is raw SQL bypassing the model, `BatchedRemoveStatusService` deliberately bypasses callbacks so any new `dependent:` must be mirrored, counter caches drift if create/destroy paths change. Risk levels are clearly motivated.

uncertainty.rationale: Implicit hedging via the "Must re-verify" list, but no explicit flagging of which dependents the answer is least sure about (e.g., FASP hooks, the full dependent-association graph).

relationship.rationale: Risks are tied to specific routes: `kept` default scope + discard column connects to `with_discarded` sites; `dependent: :destroy/:nullify/:delete` graph connects to the `delete_all` bypass in batched/delete-account services; federation risk routes through `StatusReachFinder` loading reblogs/favourites before delete. Chains are explicit, not just a ranked file list.

map_quality.rationale: Delivers a complete map: model + concerns to edit with line numbers, dependents grouped by every area (teardown/feeds/federation/notifications/moderation/lifecycle/import-export/maintenance/dependent models), and specs/fabricators to update. A teammate could land the change from this alone with only small lookups.

specificity.rationale: Files and insertion points are concrete throughout: `app/models/status.rb:66,:128,:89-118`, `safe_reblog_insert.rb:20-72`, spec paths like `spec/services/fan_out_on_write_service_spec.rb`, and even names the Fabrication fabricators explicitly rather than 'edit the model and add tests'.

justification.rationale: Each file's presence is implicitly tied to an area (teardown, federation, etc.) and the blast_radius_ranked block explains WHY edits matter (default_scope alters every unscoped query, callback-bypassing delete_all, counter-cache drift). Could be slightly more explicit per spec file.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags conditional edits with 'if ... changes' (visibility, snapshot, association graph) and 'add if missing' for concern specs, but does not call out which dependents or specs it is least sure about.

relationship.rationale: Connects most edits to the contract via stated relations: concerns to Status, `CACHEABLE_ASSOCIATIONS` linkage, callback-bypass mirror in BatchedRemoveStatusService:34 and DeleteAccountService:160-205, and pairs guards to dependents in `identity_visibility_guards`. A few dependents (e.g. background_maintenance entries) are listed without a per-item route.
